The '''MN-2E general maintenance droid''' was created to clean ceilings high above the reach of most. They were about 1 meter tall and had four arms, as well a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engine. They were used in extravagant buildings such as the great [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], where they polished windows and trimmed tree branches. They were used in most [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] installations, including [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] and the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]; they were also used by civilians. They performed detailed, delicate, and precision repair work with their sensitive [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulators]] and magnifying visual [[scanner]]s. They were equipped with low-power cutting and welding [[Laser/Legends|lasers]].

{{Droid-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Maintenance1.jpg|thumb|left|Alternate interpretation of the MN-2E as a treaded droid.]]
''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' at one point mistakenly calls this model the "MN-2D."

While ''The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook'' and ''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' interpret the MN-2E as a floating, non-humanoid model, the ''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' described and illustrated the model as roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] and moving on treaded wheels. The illustration of this model used in the ''Death Star Technical Companion'' was used in the ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' to represent the [[M38-series explorer droid]].
